title: Word Definitions, Tokenization, Boundaries, and Offsets · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs word definitions, tokenization, boundaries, and offsets from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for tested word and token contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define a word for the stakeholder, language set, punctuation, identifiers, numbers, contractions, and hyphenated forms before counting.
Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.
Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Token Invariants: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Bookbot team handles case 3196 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to tokenize under a named word policy with nonempty tokens and valid source offsets; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce nonempty tokens, monotonic nonoverlapping spans, declared normalization, and total-count reconciliation.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Token Invariants.
steps.why: Retrieving Token Invariants through the concrete tested word and token contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Token Invariants, keep Token Invariants connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Enforce nonempty tokens, monotonic nonoverlapping spans, declared normalization, and total-count reconciliation.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Token Invariants, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Token Invariants precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Token Invariants, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Bookbot team handles case 3196 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to tokenize under a named word policy with nonempty tokens and valid source offsets; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce nonempty tokens, monotonic nonoverlapping spans, declared normalization, and total-count reconciliation.

For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Token Invariants, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: If no exception occurs, tokenizer output is structurally valid.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Word Definition to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Word Definition.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using enforce and nonempty plus specific case evidence.
